site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Magic Round setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 11 Magic Round preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: magic round starts tomorrow and the board needs a calmer bounce-back than the last couple of chaotic weeks
the mark hughes foundation ledger still sits at 2 and 2 and minus $3.50 so there is no need to force a charity play
the knights look like one of the cleanest team-list reads of the round against the still-winless dragons
latrell mitchell is back for south sydney which makes rabbitohs versus dolphins a proper coin-flip mood check
the warriors welcome back key middle troops while reynolds returns for brisbane in one of the trickiest sunday games
storm have finally stopped the skid but still do not deserve automatic trust
no round 11 charity bet is locked yet

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Knights head to head looks like the cleanest pure tipping read
Newcastle keep their winning spine together against a Dragons side still searching for traction
Kalyn Ponga and Bradman Best stay central to the best upside on the card
This is the sort of game we should win on paper before trying to get clever elsewhere

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Rabbitohs versus Dolphins looks volatile now that Latrell Mitchell is back
Warriors versus Broncos has real swing-game energy with both sides regaining troops
Roosters versus Cowboys swung once Tom Dearden was ruled out and Sutton was asked to debut at five-eighth
Storm still sit in the prove-it bucket even after breaking the losing run
Do not let Magic Round chaos push us into a forced charity bet

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Take the obvious edges and avoid manufacturing upset theatre
See whether the Roosters can absorb the Radley loss better than the Cowboys absorb life without Tom Dearden
Track whether Manly can punish the Tigers without Turbo but with Fogarty back
Keep Penrith in the trust circle until a serious side finally knocks them off

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Sharks look upgraded with Mulitalo and Ramien back in the mix
The Bulldogs get Burton and Max King back which stops Friday from being a walkover
Reynolds returning matters for Brisbane but not enough to make Sunday comfortable
The Eels still look like a side worth fading unless they prove otherwise

betCard.title: Round 11 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: Keep the stake in the holster unless a price opens cleanly on Friday or Saturday
why: Magic Round can get weird quickly, so discipline matters more than forcing action.
There are a few interesting reads but no obvious charity number from the official team-list picture alone.
The right move is to lock tips first and only bet if the market offers a clean edge.
risks: Last week showed how fast a reasonable read can turn into another hit on the ledger.
Several of this week's best games are also the messiest games to price cleanly.
Betting without a solid market edge would be process drift, not aggression.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Round 10: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.

watchlist: Whether the Rabbitohs price shortens sharply with Latrell Mitchell back in the centres.
How much confidence to give the Warriors once Barnett and Egan are back on deck.
Whether the Roosters can keep the six-game streak rolling even with Victor Radley suspended.
Any sign that Round 11 still presents a disciplined $5 charity spot before Saturday.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Magic Round is a reset, but not a free-for-all
lines: As of Wednesday, 13 May 2026, official Magic Round team lists are in and the board can settle around actual personnel rather than guesswork.
The charity ledger remains at 2-2 and -$3.50, so the process should stay disciplined instead of trying to get square in one weekend.
Round 11 has plenty of edge, but several of the sharpest games also look the least predictable.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: No charity bet yet is a feature, not a bug
lines: This round does not present an obvious charity lock from the official team-list picture alone, so patience is part of the strategy.
Knights and Panthers look cleaner as tipping plays than as likely worthwhile betting prices.
Sharks and Warriors are still interesting football reads, while the Roosters now look like the clearer tipping side against a Dearden-less Cowboys outfit.
The right move is to keep the stake at $5 if a bet appears, not drift higher just because we want the bank moving again.

sections.title: Round 11 Tips
sections.heading: Magic Round tip card before lockout

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the upgrade or downgrade this week
lines: Hot: Panthers, Knights, and the healthier version of Cronulla.
Cold: Bulldogs, Eels, and any blind faith that Melbourne are suddenly fixed forever.
The biggest coin-flip game on the board is Rabbitohs versus Dolphins because both sides have enough attack to make the form line wobble.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before any charity lock
lines: Latrell Mitchell returning matters a lot for South Sydney's ceiling and makes Friday night much less comfortable for Dolphins backers.
Reynolds is back for Brisbane, but the Warriors also welcome back major middle and spine help, which keeps Sunday tricky.
The Sharks regain strike through Ronaldo Mulitalo and Jesse Ramien, while the Bulldogs also get Matt Burton and Max King back.
Tom Dearden out, Liam Sutton debuting, and Taumalolo being rested all push Roosters versus Cowboys back toward Sydney.
The current priority is getting the tips right before trying to squeeze value out of the market.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
